assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md preserves the existing Security Configuration section with Product Lifecycle, Version Streams, and Source Repositories unchanged
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md lines 42-64 contain the full Security Configuration section with all three subsections (Product Lifecycle, Version Streams, Source Repositories) exactly matching the reference file lines 52-73. All values are identical: Product pages URL (https://access.example.com/product-lifecycle), Jira version prefix (MYPRODUCT), Vulnerability issue type ID (10200), Component label pattern (pscomponent:), VEX Justification custom field (customfield_12345), Version Stream 2.1.x row, and both Source Repositories (backend, frontend-ui).

assertion_results.text: outputs/discovery-log.md reports that Security Configuration is already fully configured or up to date
assertion_results.evidence: discovery-log.md lines 32-39 state: 'Already fully configured — section exists with all subsections populated', enumerates all subsections (Product Lifecycle: all 5 fields present, Version Streams: 1 stream configured, Source Repositories: 2 repositories configured), and concludes 'No changes needed'.

assertion_results.text: outputs/changes-log.md indicates Security Configuration was preserved with no changes needed
assertion_results.evidence: changes-log.md lines 24-26 state: '## Security Configuration' / '- Preserved unchanged (Product Lifecycle, Version Streams, Source Repositories all fully populated)' / '- No changes needed'. The summary on lines 28-30 also confirms: 'No changes were made. All sections of the Project Configuration were already fully configured and were preserved as-is.'

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT offer the Security Configuration opt-in prompt — it detects the section already exists and is fully populated (idempotency skip)
assertion_results.evidence: discovery-log.md line 37 explicitly states: 'Security Configuration opt-in prompt not shown — section already exists and is fully populated'. This confirms the idempotency skip behavior. No output file contains any indication of an opt-in prompt being offered.

assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md lines 4-8 contain the Repository Registry table with both entries exactly matching the reference: 'backend | Rust backend service | serena_backend | /home/user/backend' and 'frontend-ui | TypeScript frontend | serena_ui | /home/user/frontend-ui'. discovery-log.md line 11 confirms: 'Already configured with 2 repositories: backend (serena_backend), frontend-ui (serena_ui)'.
